messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
What is the hometown of the director that won the MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker in 1992 with a movie starring Ice Cube and Cuba Gooding Jr

content.message:
I need to find the hometown of a director who won the MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker in 1992 and whose movie starred both Ice Cube and Cuba Gooding Jr.

Let me break this down step by step:

1. First, I need to identify the movie that won the MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker in 1992 and starred both Ice Cube and Cuba Gooding Jr.

2. Then, I need to find the director of that movie.

3. Finally, I need to find the hometown of that director.

Let me start by searching for movies from 1992 that won the MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker and starred both Ice Cube and Cuba Gooding Jr.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity

args.query: MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "Wikimedia category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
2. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "is a"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
3. Google Knowledge Graph ID (wd:P2671 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "kgmid"), also known as "Google knowledge panel", "Google Knowledge Panel ID", "kgmid", "MREID":
  - identifier for Google Knowledge Graph API, starting with "/g/". For IDs starting with "/m/", use Freebase ID (P646)
4. image (wd:P18 as wdt/p, matched via "photo"), also known as "album artwork", "album cover", "artwork", "camera picture", "Commons image", etc.:
  - image of relevant illustration of the subject; if available, ... map, flag image, signature image, logo image, collage image)
5. time of discovery or invention (wd:P575 as wdt/p, matched via "discovered"), also known as "date discovered", "date of discovery", "date of invention", "discovered", "discovered on", etc.:
  - date or point in time when the item was discovered or invented
6. constellation (wd:P59 as wdt/p, matched via "P59"), also known as "part of constellation":
  - the area of the celestial sphere of which the subject is a part (from a scientific standpoint, not an astrological one)
7. discoverer or inventor (wd:P61 as wdt/p, matched via "inventor"), also known as "created by", "developed by", "developer", "devised by", "discovered by", etc.:
  - subject who discovered, first described, invented, or developed this discovery or invention or scientific hypothesis or theory
8. Commons category (wd:P373 as wdt/p, matched via "Wikimedia Commons category"), also known as "category Commons", "category on Commons", "category on Wikimedia Commons", "category Wikipedia Commons", "commonscat", etc.:
  - name of the Wikimedia Commons category containing files related to this item (without the prefix "Category:")
9. right ascension component of proper motion (wd:P10752 as wdt/p, matched via "P10752"):
  - the value μᵅ as a component of the proper motion of a star
10. catalog code (wd:P528 as wdt/p, matched via "cat#"), also known as "astronomical catalog", "cat#", "catalog number", "catalogue code", "catalogue number", etc.:
  - catalog name of an object, use with qualifier P972

content.message: I need to search for the specific MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker in 1992. Let me try searching for that award directly.

tool_calls.name: search_property

args.query: MTV Movie Award for Best New Filmmaker 1992

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. nominee (wd:P2453 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award nominee"), also known as "award nominee":
  - qualifier used with “nominated for” to specify which person or organization was nominated
2. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award won"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
3. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "prizewinner"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
4. AIWARDS ID (wd:P8809 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Adult Industry Awards Database ID"), also known as "Adult Industry Awards Database ID", "AIWARDS identifier":
  - identifier for a pornographic actor, award, film or film studio in the Adult Industry Awards Database
5. European Film Awards ID (wd:P8800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - numerical identifier for a film on the European Film Awards website
6. music video (wd:P6718 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "MV"), also known as "has music video", "music clip", "MV", "song video", "videoclip":
  - short film integrating this composition with imagery
7. competition judge (wd:P12484 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "television jury"), also known as "judges", "reality television judge", "reality TV judge", "television judge", "television jury", etc.:
  - competition judge for an award or television show
8. nominated for (wd:P1411 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "nominated for award"), also known as "finalist for", "finalist for award", "have nomination to", "nominated for award", "nomination received", etc.:
  - award nomination received by a person, organisation or creative work (inspired from "award received" (Property:P166))
9. Abandonware-France award ID (wd:P12262 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "AF award ID"), also known as "A-F award ID", "AF award ID":
  - identifier of a video game award in the Abandonware France database
10. trophy awarded (wd:P4622 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "cup awarded"), also known as "cup awarded", "diploma awarded", "medal awarded":
  - trophy awarded at the end of a selection process or of a ... or finishers under the form of a cup, a medal, a diploma
